commands.review_summary:
The PR adds cron-triggered memory-core `before_agent_reply` interception for legacy light and REM dreaming tokens.

PR surface: Source +11. Total +11 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. live cron reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection supports the reported stale `agentTurn.message` legacy job shape and the PR's silent-no-op risk, but real after-fix cron output is still missing.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a concrete compatibility repair plus contributor or maintainer after-fix runtime proof, so this remains human-handled rather than a ClawSweeper repair marker while proof is absent.

target.title: fix(memory-core): intercept legacy dreaming tokens in before_agent_reply hook
target.body:
Closes #97475

## What Problem This Solves

Legacy dreaming cron tokens (`__openclaw_memory_core_light_sleep__` and `__openclaw_memory_core_rem_sleep__`) were not recognized by the `before_agent_reply` hook handler. When the cron job fired, the message containing the double-underscore-wrapped token reached the LLM. With `lightContext: true` stripping all bootstrap context files, the LLM had no guidance and attempted to execute the token as a shell command, producing `zsh: command not found` errors on every dreaming cycle (20+ consecutive cycles reported in #97475).

## Why This Change Was Made

The `before_agent_reply` hook in `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts` already recognizes managed dreaming tokens and returns `{ handled: true }` to skip LLM invocation. This fix adds detection for the legacy token variants (`LEGACY_LIGHT_SLEEP_EVENT_TEXT` / `LEGACY_REM_SLEEP_EVENT_TEXT`) to the same guard clause, restricted to `ctx.trigger === "cron"` to avoid false positives in interactive sessions.

The fix is in the hook handler — before the LLM is ever called — so no shell command execution attempt occurs.

## Evidence

### Source-level proof
- The legacy token constants (`LEGACY_LIGHT_SLEEP_EVENT_TEXT`, `LEGACY_REM_SLEEP_EVENT_TEXT`) are defined in `extensions/memory-core/src/constants.ts` and match the exact text that appears in system events from legacy cron jobs
- The check uses `includesSystemEventToken` (the same function that detects the managed tokens), ensuring consistent matching behavior
- The `cron` trigger guard ensures interactive sessions are never affected

### Code review
- `git diff --check`: passed
- The change is +11 lines, one file only

### Not tested
- Live cron dreaming cycle (requires a running gateway with memory-core plugin and cron scheduling)

## Merge Risk

**Low**. The change:
- Only intercepts already-known legacy token strings
- Restricted to `ctx.trigger === "cron"` (no effect on interactive sessions)
- Returns `{ handled: true }` (the same signal used by managed dreaming tokens)
- No external dependencies or config changes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
